requirements.task_name: Open the most cited CC BY neuroscience article published between 2021–2022 and view its PDF
requirements.task_description: Search for neuroscience articles, filter by publication years 2021–2022 and CC BY license, sort by citation count, and open the PDF of the most cited article.

required_variables.set_condition: Each time searchArticles is called, AFTER filtering and sorting, if all of the following hold: (1) queryString (lowercased) contains the token "neuroscience"; (2) publicationYearMin === 2021; (3) publicationYearMax === 2022; (4) accessTypes is an array that includes "open_access"; (5) licenses is an array that includes "cc_by"; (6) sortOption === "citations_desc".
required_variables.value_to_set: A JSON-serializable object such as { queryString, publicationYearMin, publicationYearMax, accessTypes, licenses, sortOption, timestamp: this._now() }
required_variables.reason: Records that the user executed a neuroscience search with the correct year range, access type, license, and sort order so the evaluator can verify correct filter usage.

required_variables.set_condition: Under the same condition as task1_searchParams (correct neuroscience search); after pagination and before returning, if paginated.results[0] exists.
required_variables.value_to_set: paginated.results[0].id (or an empty string if no result exists)
required_variables.reason: Captures which article is actually the top-cited result for the correctly filtered/sorted neuroscience search at the time the search was run.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getArticleDetails(articleId) is called and the corresponding article exists.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object representing a JSON-encoded array of unique article IDs; e.g., read the existing array from localStorage (or []), append articleId if not already present, and write it back.
required_variables.reason: Tracks which articles’ detail pages were opened. The evaluator can check that the article whose ID equals task1_topArticleId is among these, confirming that the user opened the most cited qualifying article.

requirements.task_name: Create a reading list of 3 highly cited climate change articles in Environmental Science from the last 5 years
requirements.task_description: Find climate change impact articles in Environmental Science from 2019–2024, select those with more than 50 citations, and save 3 of them into a dedicated reading list.

requirements.task_name: Choose a computer science journal for submission with APC ≤ $1500 and review time ≤ 60 days and open its submission guidelines
requirements.task_description: Browse computer science journals, compare APC and median review time for the first three, select the first journal that meets the APC and review time thresholds, and open its submission guidelines page.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getJournalDetails(journalId) is called and the journal exists.
required_variables.value_to_set: A JSON-encoded array of unique journal IDs; read the current array (or []), add journalId if it is not already present, and save back.
required_variables.reason: Captures which journals the user opened for inspection so the evaluator can confirm that at least several journals (e.g., the top three in computer science) were considered during the comparison step.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getSubmissionGuidelines(journalId) is called (regardless of whether guidelines are found).
required_variables.reason: Identifies the journal whose submission guidelines the user attempted to open. Using journal metadata, the evaluator can verify that this journal has APC ≤ 1500, medianReviewTimeDays ≤ 60, and is the correct choice among the compared journals.

requirements.task_name: Export APA citations for the two largest-sample cardiology randomized controlled trials
requirements.task_description: Search for cardiology randomized controlled trials, filter to clinical trials in cardiology, sort by sample size, and copy APA citations for the two largest studies.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getArticleCitation(articleId, style) is called, the article exists, and the normalized style (style.toLowerCase()) is "apa".
required_variables.value_to_set: A JSON-encoded array of unique article IDs; read the current array (or []), add articleId if not already present, and save back.
required_variables.reason: Tracks which articles the user obtained APA-style citations for. The evaluator can then check that at least the two largest-sample cardiology clinical trials (by sampleSize) are included in this set.

requirements.task_name: From a 2023 journal issue, open a long review article and save its HTML full text to a reading list
requirements.task_description: Browse to a 2023 issue of a specific journal, locate a review article spanning at least 11 pages, open its HTML full text, and add it to a dedicated reading list.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getArticleFullTextHtml(articleId) is called and the article exists.
required_variables.value_to_set: A JSON-encoded array of unique article IDs; read the current array (or []), add articleId if not already present, and save back.
required_variables.reason: Records which articles’ HTML full text the user opened. Combined with reading_list_items and article/journal/issue metadata, the evaluator can confirm that at least one of these is a 2023 review article from the specified journal with a page span ≥ 11 pages and that it was saved into the 'Env Studies Reviews 2023' reading list.

requirements.task_name: Create a weekly search alert for recent machine learning in healthcare articles
requirements.task_description: Run a search on machine learning in healthcare, restrict results to 2020 and later in relevant subjects, sort by newest, and set up a weekly on-site alert named "ML Health Weekly".

requirements.task_name: Find and open the details page of an open CSV dataset linked from a gene expression article
requirements.task_description: Search for gene expression dataset articles with open data, open one such article, navigate to its data section, and open the details page of a CSV-format dataset, then view its file list.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getDatasetDetails(datasetId) is called and the dataset exists.
required_variables.value_to_set: A JSON-encoded array of unique dataset IDs; read the current array (or []), add datasetId if not already present, and save back.
required_variables.reason: Records which dataset detail pages were opened. Using the linked article and dataset metadata (e.g., primaryFormat or file formats via dataset_files), the evaluator can confirm that at least one opened dataset is CSV-format and linked from an article with open data.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getDatasetFiles(datasetId) is called and the dataset exists.
required_variables.value_to_set: A JSON-encoded array of unique dataset IDs; read the current array (or []), add datasetId if not already present, and save back.
required_variables.reason: Specifically tracks for which datasets the user viewed the file list. The evaluator can intersect this set with task7_openedDatasetIds and dataset metadata to verify that the file list of an appropriate CSV dataset was viewed.

requirements.task_name: Build a bibliography collection of 5 urban air pollution articles from 2018–2023 from at least 3 different journals
requirements.task_description: Search for urban air pollution health effect articles from 2018–2023, filter by relevant subjects, and add 5 articles from at least 3 distinct journals into a single collection.
